## 1. New LLM Versions / Major AI Lab Tools
- Date: 2026-04-04
  Link: https://www.businessinsider.com/anthropic-cuts-off-openclaw-support-claude-subscriptions-2026-4
  Summary: Anthropic said Claude subscriptions will no longer cover third-party agent tools such as OpenClaw; affected users will need separate API keys or paid extra-usage bundles because this usage places an outsized load on systems.
  Why it matters: This is a direct operating-policy shift from a frontier lab around agentic usage, with immediate impact on autonomous workflow economics and reliability planning.

## 4. Major AI Data Center News
- Date: 2026-04-05
  Link: https://m.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/foxconn-firstquarter-revenue-jumps-30-yy-4597591?ampMode=1
  Summary: Foxconn reported Q1 revenue up 29.7% year over year, citing strong AI product demand, especially in cloud and networking.
  Why it matters: This is a supply-chain readout showing continued hard demand for AI infrastructure rather than only software-side enthusiasm.

## 5. Regulation & Policy
- Date: 2026-04-05
  Link: https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/technology/articles/britain-woos-expansion-effort-anthropic-042916627.html
  Summary: Reuters reported the UK is trying to persuade Anthropic to expand its British footprint, including options such as a larger London presence or dual listing, after Anthropic’s dispute with the US Defense Department.
  Why it matters: Governments are visibly competing for frontier AI lab presence, making AI policy increasingly tied to industrial strategy and geopolitical leverage.
